phases.name: Composed proxy walking skeleton (BLOCKING integration gate)

steps.name: Composed bidirectional proxy walking skeleton over real netns/veth with NO RST
steps.description: The BLOCKING first DELIVER slice (F2). Compose the spike-proven primitives into ONE bidirectional walking skeleton over real netns/veth with cgroup-isolated workloads, closing the three gaps the spikes left open. This is an integration gate, not a prove-the-mechanism gate. It MUST pass before any other slice lands.
criteria: GAP 1 -- outbound composed in ONE flow: a workload's intercepted connect() drains pre-arm plaintext losslessly, completes the agent's TLS 1.3 client handshake presenting the held SVID, and the steady-state forward leg carries the bytes with NO RST [anchor: findings-userspace-relay.md + findings-egress-ktls-splice.md, composed; ADR-0069 'Composed walking-skeleton gate']

GAP 2 -- bidirectional steady-state round-trip: post-arm transfer is multi-record in BOTH directions (outbound forward + return, inbound request + the S->C response leg the spikes never composed), NO RST [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md 'What was NOT tested' + findings-splice-return.md]

GAP 3 -- real topology: the composed flow runs over real netns/veth with cgroup-isolated workloads (NOT loopback + sibling processes); the intercepts and splices all hold [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md 'What was NOT tested']

The peer-facing leg shows TLS 1.3 records (tcpdump 0x17) in both directions; the workload's plaintext appears ONLY on the host-internal leg, never on the peer leg [anchor: findings-egress-ktls-splice.md Assertion 1 / findings-inbound-intercept.md §3]

Gate: cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-dataplane --features integration-tests is green for the composed acceptance test on the real 6.18 kernel, ACTUALLY EXECUTING (NOT --no-run)

production_files: crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mtls_enforcement.rs (the accepted MtlsEnforcement port contract verbatim, minimal stub bodies sufficient to compose the skeleton)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs (HostMtlsEnforcement adapter skeleton)
crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_connect4_mtls.rs (outbound intercept, extends cgroup_connect4_service)
crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/sk_skb_stream_verdict_mtls.rs (forward sockmap egress-redirect)
test_files: crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/helpers/mtls_netns_topology.rs (real netns/veth + cgroup-isolated workload fixtures; promote to overdrive-testing if a second consumer appears)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 ONLY for the gate: a composed acceptance test on the real 6.18 Lima/LVH kernel -- sockops/cgroup_connect4/TPROXY/kTLS have NO meaningful BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN, so there is no Tier-2 backstop and the firing-scope can only be settled at Tier 3 (per .claude/rules/testing.md). Run via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run ... --features integration-tests`; a --no-run gate is green even when every fixture refuses at boot, so the composed intercept lifecycle MUST actually execute. The increment-e harness intercept-lifecycle RST is a throwaway-harness artifact, NOT a kernel finding -- defeated by the production intercept lifecycle (findings-userspace-relay.md Crux 2). The agent reads SVID + bundle ONLY via IdentityRead (a reader, #35), and kTLS arms on the agent's leg, never the workload's socket; expected_peer is None in v1. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT (feature-delta MtlsEnforcement section) verbatim -- do NOT invent public API; if composing appears to need an unnamed primitive, that is a BLOCKER to surface, not surface to add (CLAUDE.md 'Implement to the design'). Mutation testing is not meaningful for a Tier-3-only composition gate (no default-lane logic surface yet); contract types and pure helpers get mutation coverage as 02-01 onward exercises them. RED scaffold (#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]) only if landing the test ahead of the adapter wiring. Integration tests gated behind the `integration-tests` feature per workspace convention.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch DELIVER reference (retained 2026-06-11). This slice COMPOSES four proven increments — read them for the exact working syscall sequences: spike-scratch/increment-e-userspace-relay/ (cgroup_connect4 intercept + lossless pre-arm capture + handshake-window flush; relay/src/{main,agent}.rs, bpf/src/main.rs); spike-scratch/increment-f-egress-ktls-splice/ (forward agent-idle egress sockmap redirect into kTLS-TX; bpf/src/main.rs = sk_skb/stream_verdict, agent/src/main.rs = kTLS arm); spike-scratch/increment-h-splice-return/agent/src/main.rs (return agent-light splice(2)); spike-scratch/increment-i-inbound-intercept/agent/src/main.rs (composed inbound flow). The step productionises their composition in real netns/veth.

phases.name: Productionised happy path: intercept, handshake, outbound enforce

steps.name: Transparent intercept + leg-acquire (outbound rewrite + inbound TPROXY) with no recursion
steps.description: Productionise the intercept + leg-acquire for BOTH directions: outbound connect()-rewrite to the agent's leg-F listener with lossless pre-arm capture, inbound TPROXY redirect to an IP_TRANSPARENT leg-C listener with original-destination recovery, and the agent-private intercept-exemption mechanism so the agent's own peer dial is not re-intercepted. The F5 negative proofs are deferred to 04-01.
criteria: An intercepted outbound connection is routed to the agent's leg-F listener and its pre-arm plaintext is captured LOSSLESSLY (no dropped bytes) into a bounded buffer [anchor: findings-userspace-relay.md Unknown 1 -- route by local_port only; a local_ip4 byte-order disagreement was found]

An intercepted inbound connection is redirected to the agent's IP_TRANSPARENT leg-C listener and the original destination is recovered via getsockname (NOT SO_ORIGINAL_DST) [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §1 (ORIG_DST=127.0.0.2:18443 recovered)]

The agent's own peer-facing dial is NOT re-intercepted by the workload intercept program -- the bypass is agent-private and scoped to the agent, not the workload cgroup subtree [anchor: ADR-0069 'intercept-recursion / agent-leg-B exemption']
Intercept + listener setup succeed under the agent's privilege (CAP_NET_ADMIN); the workload is unprivileged and holds nothing [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md Mechanics #1]

An intercepted connection (either direction) is accepted, associated with the correct allocation, and enforceable through the MtlsEnforcement port without re-deriving routing from an unsafe socket tuple (SD-1 (a): the owned leg + direction-tagged routing fact; no pidfd, no 4-tuple re-derivation)

production_files: crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/cgroup_connect4_mtls.rs (outbound connect()-rewrite + the agent-private intercept-exemption bypass)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/intercept.rs (leg-F lossless pre-arm capture; inbound IP_TRANSPARENT listener + getsockname orig-dst recovery; TPROXY setup)
crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mtls_enforcement.rs (Direction / Routed / InterceptedConnection finalised from the 01-01 stub)
test_files: crates/overdrive-core/tests/mtls_enforcement_newtypes.rs (proptest roundtrip ONLY for any newtype with a stable public serialization contract -- e.g. Direction/Routed Display/FromStr/serde, per the mandatory newtype call site; NOT a shape test of InterceptedConnection's owned-fd payload)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 for the intercept/orig-dst/lossless-capture observables (real cgroup_connect4 rewrite + TPROXY + getsockname on the real kernel; no BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN for cgroup_sock_addr / TPROXY contexts). Tier 1 / proptest is scoped to newtypes that carry a stable public serialization or value contract (the newtype-roundtrip mandatory call site) -- Direction / Routed where they FromStr/Display/serde. Do NOT write standalone unit tests of internal predicates (the local_port routing decision, the SO_MARK/cgroup bypass check-and-skip): exercise those through the driving boundary (the intercept acceptance test) and the DST equivalence harness, with the Tier-3 wire/orig-dst observables as the acceptance signal. Route the relay by local_port ONLY (the Unknown-1 byte-order trap). Mutation testing >=80% kill-rate diff-scoped (cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main, Lima-prefixed because integration-tests participate) on the touched default-lane logic. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- the InterceptedConnection payload is SD-1 (a); do NOT add fields or a pidfd. The F5 negative proofs are 04-01 scope -- only the bypass mechanism lands here. Integration tests gated behind `integration-tests`; RED scaffold convention for any test landing ahead of impl.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch reference: spike-scratch/increment-e-userspace-relay/ (cgroup_connect4 rewrite + orig-dst + lossless leg-F pre-arm capture — relay/src/{main,agent}.rs, bpf/src/main.rs) and spike-scratch/increment-i-inbound-intercept/ (TPROXY/IP_TRANSPARENT intercept + getsockname orig-dst — agent/src/main.rs; run-intercept-probe.sh isolates it). Exact intercept + leg-acquire mechanics to productionise.

steps.name: Agent mutual-TLS handshake presenting the held SVID (client leg B + server leg C)
steps.description: On the agent-acquired leg, the agent completes a TLS 1.3 mutual handshake presenting the workload's own held SVID (read via IdentityRead) and verifying the peer chains to the trust bundle -- client role outbound, REQUIRE+VERIFY server role inbound. Both workloads remain identity-unaware (no cert, no key). This slice ends at a completed handshake with secrets ready; the bytes are not yet kTLS.
criteria: Outbound (client): the agent completes a TLS 1.3 client handshake on the peer-facing leg presenting the held SVID and verifying the peer's server cert chains to the trust bundle [anchor: findings.md A]

Inbound (server): the agent completes a TLS 1.3 server handshake on the client-facing leg presenting the server SVID and requiring-and-verifying the client's SVID chains to the bundle (REQUIRE+VERIFY); a valid client cert -> handshake succeeds [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §2]
The presented leaf chains to the root and its SAN is the workload's SPIFFE URI (spiffe://overdrive.local/job/<name>/alloc/<id>) -- provable from the captured handshake at the test tier
Both workloads hold no cert and no key -- the leaf material stays with the agent (read via the identity port); the workloads are identity-unaware

The enforcement adapter takes the identity read-port as a REQUIRED constructor parameter (never defaulted, never a builder override), per port-trait discipline [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md Mechanics #3/#6 for the server-config mechanics]

production_files: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/handshake.rs (rustls client/server config backed by an identity-read cert resolver; REQUIRE+VERIFY against the trust bundle; the two pinned server-config mechanics)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs (HostMtlsEnforcement holds the identity read-port + limits as REQUIRED constructor params; the enforce handshake phase, dispatching on Direction)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 for the real mutual-TLS handshake on the real kernel (client + server roles; captured handshake proving leaf-chains-to-root + SPIFFE-SAN). The identity read is the shipped-port integration seam (#35); None is the fail-closed signal (exercised in detail in 04-01). Tier 1 DST: the SimMtlsEnforcement double models the handshake OUTCOME (Established vs fail-closed reason) driven by a preloaded SimIdentityRead; the equivalence harness drives both adapters through the same sequence for BOTH directions. Express the cert-resolver selection and the constructor-param plumbing through the driving boundary (the handshake acceptance test + the equivalence harness), NOT as standalone unit tests of the selection predicate. Mutation testing >=80% on the default-lane logic the handshake phase introduces. The two server-config mechanics (NewSessionTicket suppression; read peer certificates BEFORE consuming the connection's secrets) are pinned by findings-inbound-intercept.md Mechanics #3/#6, not improvised. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- the identity port is a REQUIRED constructor param (never defaulted, never a with_identity builder). Do NOT mint/re-issue/cache the SVID -- #26 is a READER. RED scaffold convention; integration-tests feature gate.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch reference: spike-scratch/increment-f-egress-ktls-splice/agent/src/main.rs (rustls ClientConfig + secret extraction, client leg) and spike-scratch/increment-i-inbound-intercept/agent/src/main.rs (rustls ServerConfig + client-auth verifier REQUIRE+VERIFY, server leg; ticket suppression); spike-scratch/increment-a/src/main.rs for the minimal rustls->kTLS arm. Working handshake + secret-extraction code.

steps.name: OUTBOUND enforce: kTLS-TX arm, forward agent-idle splice + return agent-light splice, TLS 1.3 on the wire
steps.description: After the outbound handshake, the agent installs the extracted secrets into kTLS on its peer-facing leg and hands steady state to the kernel: the forward direction is agent-idle (in-kernel sockmap egress redirect, zero per-byte syscalls), the return direction is agent-light (a port-owned bounded splice pump). The peer wire carries TLS 1.3 records; the arming-order invariant is pinned at Tier 3. The pump is port-owned (SD-2).
criteria: The agent installs the handshake's extracted secrets into kTLS on the peer-facing leg -- the auth session IS the data session, not a separately negotiated one [anchor: findings.md A / findings-egress-ktls-splice.md mechanic #4]

Forward agent-idle: tcpdump on the peer wire shows TLS 1.3 records (0x17) and strace shows the agent issues ZERO per-byte syscalls (redir_err=0) [anchor: findings-egress-ktls-splice.md (15/15 deterministic; strace proof)]

Return agent-light: strace shows ONLY splice/ppoll (zero payload read/write), byte-exact plaintext on the host-internal leg, ~1 splice per TLS record, einval_on_B=0 [anchor: findings-splice-return.md]

ss -tie shows the kTLS ULP on the peer-facing leg (tcp-ulp-tls 1.3 aes-gcm-256 rxconf:sw txconf:sw) -- NOT ss -K [anchor: findings.md A]; the peer wire NEVER carries cleartext (cleartext count on that leg = 0), the K1 North-Star observable [anchor: findings-egress-ktls-splice.md Assertion 1]

(Tier-3 invariant) a SOCKMAP insert AFTER TCP_ULP "tls" returns EINVAL (the natural reverse order passes) -- surfaces as ArmingOrderViolation; enforce returns with liveness == Running and the return pump is PORT-OWNED (SD-2) [anchor: findings.md Increment D / D-MTLS-7]

production_files: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/ktls_arm.rs (extracted-secrets -> kTLS install on the peer leg; the arming order; control-record handling via ktls::KtlsStream)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/splice_pump.rs (port-owned return-splice pump on a plain no-psock kTLS-RX leg; bytes-spliced progress counter for liveness)
crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/sk_skb_stream_verdict_mtls.rs (forward sockmap egress-redirect, finalised from the 01-01 skeleton)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs (HostMtlsEnforcement::enforce Outbound steady-state install + liveness + teardown)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 + Tier 4. Tier 3: kTLS arm + forward sockmap-egress-redirect + return splice on the real kernel; wire observables (tcpdump 0x17, ss -tie ULP, strace agent-idle forward / agent-light return). Tier 4: the new sk_skb/stream_verdict BPF program lands a verifier-budget baseline (perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget); verifier-regress applies, xdp-perf is N/A (sockmap, not XDP). Tier 2 has NO meaningful BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN for sockops/sk_skb -- those move to Tier 3. Tier 1 DST: SimMtlsEnforcement models the steady-state OUTCOME + liveness==Running; the equivalence harness asserts the observable trajectory. Express the arming-order sequencing and the splice-pump progress accounting through the driving boundary (the Tier-3 wire/cost-tier observables + the liveness surface + the equivalence harness), NOT as standalone unit tests of the predicates. Mutation testing >=80% on the default-lane logic (the liveness derivation, any pure arming/accounting helpers). Control records (NewSessionTicket/KeyUpdate -> EIO on raw kTLS RX) handled via ktls::KtlsStream's control-message loop, NOT raw setsockopt (D-MTLS-8); the return leg carries NO psock on RX (D-MTLS-5) -- the plain-kTLS-RX invariant the splice depends on. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- the pump is PORT-OWNED (SD-2 (a)), no pump_once method; enforce dispatches on Direction (one method), no enforce_outbound. RED scaffold convention; integration-tests gate.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch reference: spike-scratch/increment-f-egress-ktls-splice/ (bpf/src/main.rs = sk_skb/stream_verdict egress redirect flags=0; agent/src/main.rs = kTLS-TX arm + SOCKMAP-insert-before-TCP_ULP ordering + agent-idle; run-idle-proof.sh = the strace agent-idle evidence) and spike-scratch/increment-h-splice-return/agent/src/main.rs (return agent-light splice(2) pump). Exact working outbound steady-state.

steps.name: INBOUND enforce: orig-dst -> server-mTLS -> kTLS-RX -> agent-light splice-to-server
steps.description: After the inbound intercept selects the server workload's identity from the recovered original destination, the agent completes the server-side mutual-TLS handshake, arms kTLS-RX on its client-facing leg, and splices the decrypted plaintext to the identity-unaware server workload (agent-light) -- the server reads the byte-exact request while the client wire carries ciphertext only. The deliver pump is the same port-owned splice primitive the outbound return uses. The loopback-only spike is re-proven in real netns/veth.
criteria: Orig-dst -> identity: the recovered original destination selects the server workload's allocation and its held SVID via the identity port [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §1 + 'Design implications' #3]

Server-mTLS: the agent presents the server SVID and REQUIRE+VERIFY the client's SVID chains to the bundle; a valid client cert -> handshake succeeds, kTLS-RX armed (ss -tie rxconf:sw) [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §2/§3]

Byte-exact plaintext to the server: the server reads the byte-exact request as plaintext; the client-facing leg carries 0x17 app_data only (cleartext-hits on the client leg = 0); decrypted plaintext appears ONLY on the agent->server leg [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §3 (PLAINTEXT_EXACT; client leg 0x17 records=2, cleartext-hits=0)]

Agent-light: strace shows the agent moves the inbound payload via splice/ppoll only -- zero per-byte read/write/recv/send; the client-facing leg carries no psock on RX (the same plain-kTLS-RX invariant) [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §5 (splice_in=1 splice_out=1)]

The adapter owns the server-facing leg end-to-end -- the worker is never handed a TLS or server-side fd; the enforced connection reports liveness == Running and tears down to Gone; the loopback spike topology is re-proven in the real netns/veth shape [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md 'What was NOT tested']

production_files: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/inbound.rs (orig-dst -> allocation -> SVID selection via the identity port; the agent-opened leg to the server workload inside enforce; the deliver-splice pump)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/ktls_arm.rs (kTLS-RX arm on the client-facing leg, reused/extended from 02-03)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs (HostMtlsEnforcement::enforce Inbound branch dispatching on Direction::Inbound)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 for the full inbound path on the real kernel (TPROXY orig-dst + server-mTLS + kTLS-RX + agent-light splice-to-server; byte-exact plaintext at the server; client-leg 0x17-only; strace splice-only). The whole mechanism is proven end-to-end in findings-inbound-intercept.md §1-§5 (increment-i, kernel 7.0) -- the cost here is productionising the identity-selection lookup (orig-dst -> allocation -> SVID via the identity port, which the spike hardcoded) and re-proving the loopback spike topology in the real netns/veth shape. No BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN for TPROXY/cgroup_sock_addr/kTLS -> Tier 3 only. Tier 1 DST: SimMtlsEnforcement models the Inbound outcome identically to Outbound; the equivalence harness exercises enforce(Inbound, present alloc) -> Running -> teardown -> Gone. Express the orig-dst -> allocation selection and the deliver-pump accounting through the driving boundary + equivalence harness, NOT as standalone predicate unit tests. Mutation testing >=80% on the default-lane logic introduced. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- the server leg is adapter-internal (opened inside enforce, per SD-1); enforce dispatches on Direction::Inbound, no enforce_inbound; the deliver pump is PORT-OWNED (SD-2). The inbound fail-closed NEGATIVES (nocert/wrongca distinct-reason proofs) are 04-01 scope -- the verifier REQUIRE+VERIFY is wired here. RED scaffold convention; integration-tests gate.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch reference: spike-scratch/increment-i-inbound-intercept/agent/src/main.rs (the entire proven inbound path — TPROXY intercept, getsockname orig-dst, server-mTLS, kTLS-RX arm, agent-light splice-to-server; run-full.sh MODE=ok drives it) plus spike-scratch/increment-g-ktls-rx-splice/agent/src/main.rs for the kTLS-RX decrypt mechanics. The proven composed inbound implementation to productionise.

phases.name: Guardrails: fail-closed, limits, supervision, intercept-exemption, honest authn boundary

steps.name: Fail-closed (cause-distinct) + resource limits (F4/F7) + pump supervision (F6) + intercept-exemption negatives (F5) + honest authn boundary (F1)
steps.description: The security teeth: the encryption guarantee cannot be bypassed and its boundary is honest. Fail-closed cause-distinct (both directions), the F4/F7 resource limits and F6 pump supervision hold at their concrete values with no leak, the intercept cannot be self-exempted (F5), and v1 is exactly chain-to-bundle authn with NO intended-peer pinning. Authorization is #27/#38, out of scope.
criteria: Outbound fail-closed: an absent SVID -> AbsentSvid (the agent refuses, no cleartext reaches the peer, the peer leg is never armed); a peer not chaining to the bundle -> PeerVerificationFailed (no TLS app data, no cleartext) [anchor: ADR-0069 'Authn-only boundary']

Inbound fail-closed, distinct reasons: nocert ("peer sent no certificates") and wrongca ("invalid peer certificate: BadSignature") each reject with their DISTINCT reason BEFORE any splice; the server workload receives 0 bytes [anchor: findings-inbound-intercept.md §4]

Resource limits at their CONCRETE values (assert the value, not field existence): max_prearm_bytes=256 KiB -> BufferLimitExceeded (buffer dropped, leg reset, no cleartext); handshake_deadline=5 s -> HandshakeTimeout; max_inflight_per_alloc=128 -> InFlightLimitExceeded; cleanup leaks no fd/sockmap/kTLS state (re-query liveness -> Gone) [anchor: feature-delta MtlsLimits (F7 defaults); ADR-0069 'Resource & robustness constraints']

Pump supervision (F6): a pump whose bytes-spliced counter has not advanced for pump_stall_deadline=30 s while a record is pending transitions to Stalled; the worker tears the connection down (-> Gone, no fd/sockmap/kTLS leak) and emits mtls.pump.stalled / mtls.pump.teardown_on_stall [anchor: ADR-0069 ATAM 'Pump supervision policy (F6)']

Intercept-exemption negatives (F5): the agent's peer-facing dial is NOT re-intercepted (no recursion); a workload that sets the bypass on its OWN socket is STILL intercepted (agent-private, unreachable from the workload). Honest authn boundary (F1): v1 verifies chain-to-bundle ONLY (both directions); the wrong-but-valid-peer PeerIdentityMismatch negative is present but #[ignore]-gated on #178 and NOTHING calls that case 'protected' until #178 lands [anchor: ADR-0069 'intercept-recursion / agent-leg-B exemption' + Decision 'The honest v1 security claim']

production_files: crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/limits.rs (MtlsLimits enforcement: pre-arm buffer cap -> BufferLimitExceeded; handshake deadline -> HandshakeTimeout; per-alloc in-flight ceiling -> InFlightLimitExceeded; fail-closed cleanup, no leak)
crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/supervision.rs (liveness Stalled derivation from the bytes-spliced progress metric + pending-record check; the stall telemetry)
crates/overdrive-worker/src/mtls_supervisor.rs (worker reconciler-tick point-query of liveness; teardown-on-Stalled policy -- D-MTLS-10 / SD-4)
crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mtls_enforcement.rs (the cause-distinct MtlsEnforcementError variants + PumpLiveness::Stalled finalised)
test_files: crates/overdrive-sim/tests/integration/mtls_enforcement_equivalence.rs (DST equivalence harness: both adapters, both directions, fail-closed branches, the F4/F7 concrete-value limit trips, F6 stall->teardown->Gone)

steps.implementation_notes: Mixed-tier; Tier 1 DST is heavily load-bearing. The SimMtlsEnforcement double + the mtls_enforcement_equivalence harness drive BOTH adapters through the fail-closed branches (AbsentSvid via a preloaded SimIdentityRead WITHOUT the alloc; scripted HandshakeFailed/PeerVerificationFailed), the F4/F7 concrete-value limit trips (BufferLimitExceeded fires at exactly 256 KiB+1; HandshakeTimeout at 5 s; InFlightLimitExceeded at the 129th concurrent pre-arm; pump_stall_deadline==30 s -- NOT merely field existence), and the F6 stall -> worker-teardown -> Gone policy (scriptable Running->Stalled). Tier 3 for the kernel-mechanism guardrails: inbound nocert/wrongca distinct reasons (findings-inbound-intercept.md §4); a deliberately-exceeded pre-arm buffer; a stalled handshake; a paused splice pump with a pending RX record (Stalled within 30 s -> teardown -> no leak); the F5 negatives. Mutation testing >=80% on EVERY limit-trip predicate, the Stalled derivation, the cause-distinct error mapping, and the fail-closed cleanup path -- a < vs <= mutation on the 256 KiB / 128 / 30 s thresholds MUST be killed. The PeerIdentityMismatch wrong-but-valid-peer negative is present but #[ignore = "gated on #178 supplying expected_peer"] -- do NOT wire SAN-match in v1 (expected_peer stays None), and no doc/test calls that case 'protected'. Authorization (allow/deny) is #27/#38 -- the proxy MUST NOT embed a policy engine / read policy_verdicts. Operator-tunability of MtlsLimits is #230 -- surface as a blocker if it arises, create NO GH issue. IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- all error variants + PumpLiveness::Stalled are pinned; do NOT add variants. RED scaffold convention; integration-tests gate.
steps.reference_implementation: Gitignored spike-scratch reference: spike-scratch/increment-i-inbound-intercept/ (agent/src/main.rs = fail-closed nocert/wrongca rejection with distinct reasons; run-full.sh MODE=nocert|wrongca drives the negatives). MtlsLimits + pump-supervision values are design-pinned (no spike) — enforce per the accepted contract.

phases.name: Production activation (composition-root wiring; BLOCKING external-validity gate)

steps.name: Wire HostMtlsEnforcement into the production node/worker boot path; probe-then-use, fail closed; live-allocation intercepts
steps.description: The external-validity step: the production node/worker composition root constructs HostMtlsEnforcement, runs probe() at startup and FAILS CLOSED on error (no silent boot without enforcement), and attaches the intercepts for live host-socket allocations -- so a workload deployed via `overdrive deploy` is actually enforced. Without this, adapter Tier-3 tests pass while production never enables mTLS (the built-in-ca 02-02 cold-boot regression class).
criteria: The node/worker boot path constructs HostMtlsEnforcement with the identity read-port + MtlsLimits and runs probe() at startup, BEFORE serving any allocation traffic (the wire->probe->use composition-root invariant) [anchor: feature-delta MtlsEnforcement probe doc; ADR-0069 § Enforcement; D-MTLS-11]

A failed probe() refuses to start with a structured health.startup.refused event and the node does NOT degrade to a cleartext path -- fail-closed for confidentiality (mirroring the existing ebpf_dataplane.probe() refusal in run_server)
For a live host-socket allocation, the boot path attaches the cgroup_connect4 / TPROXY intercepts so the workload's connections are routed through the agent's legs once it reaches Running

END-TO-END acceptance (the load-bearing gate): a normal exec workload deployed via `overdrive deploy <SPEC>` produces TLS 1.3 records (tcpdump 0x17) on its peer-facing leg with zero payload cleartext, and the peer reads byte-exact plaintext -- proven by a driving-port / end-to-end Tier-3 test that ACTUALLY RUNS under Lima (cargo xtask lima run -- ...), NOT a compile-check / --no-run
The workload holds no SVID material (identity-unaware) and the CLI verb is `overdrive deploy <SPEC>` (NOT `job submit`); the e2e test exercises the real boot path, not the adapter in isolation

production_files: crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs (run_server composition root: construct HostMtlsEnforcement with the identity read-port + MtlsLimits, probe()-then-use with health.startup.refused on failure, mirroring the existing ebpf_dataplane.probe() refusal)
crates/overdrive-worker/src/ (node/worker startup + allocation lifecycle: attach the intercepts for live host-socket allocations once an alloc reaches Running; supervise enforced connections)
test_files: crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/mtls_production_activation.rs (end-to-end: deploy an exec workload via the real boot path and assert TLS 1.3 on its peer-facing leg)

steps.implementation_notes: Tier 3 end-to-end through the REAL production boot path. This is the step that closes the external-validity gap the review flagged (BLOCKER): no prior step wires HostMtlsEnforcement into run_server / the worker allocation lifecycle, so adapter Tier-3 tests could be green while `overdrive serve` never enables mTLS for real host-socket workloads. The acceptance gate MUST actually run under Lima (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests); a --no-run / compile-check is explicitly insufficient -- 'a --no-run gate is green even when every fixture refuses at boot' is precisely the regression this step exists to prevent (the built-in-ca-operator-composition 02-02 cold-boot precedent). The composition root follows wire->probe->use: construct the adapter (BPF objects/maps loaded+pinned), call probe(), and refuse to start on Err with a structured health.startup.refused event -- reusing the exact precedent already in run_server for ebpf_dataplane.probe(). The probe is fail-closed for confidentiality: a node that cannot honor the encryption contract MUST NOT boot serving cleartext. The worker (D-MTLS-10, in-process node agent) attaches the intercepts for live allocations and supervises the enforced connections via the liveness point-query (SD-4). IMPLEMENT TO THE ACCEPTED CONTRACT -- probe/enforce/liveness/teardown only; do NOT invent wiring surface beyond the MtlsEnforcement port + the existing run_server/worker startup shape. The operator verb is `overdrive deploy <SPEC>`; do NOT invent a CLI verb (encryption is automatic and undisableable, no driving CLI surface). Mutation testing >=80% diff-scoped on any default-lane wiring logic (the probe-result branch, the allocation-attach predicate). RED scaffold convention; integration-tests gate.
steps.reference_implementation: No spike implementation — this is NEW production composition-root wiring. Codebase precedent to mirror: overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs run_server's ebpf_dataplane.probe() -> health.startup.refused fail-closed-on-boot pattern (apply the same wire->probe->use shape for HostMtlsEnforcement::probe at startup).
